name: forge_smith
description: Inspect the forge — diagnose environment, VS Code config, setup health, version currency, and common problems. Returns structured results with pass/fail/warning counts.

name: forge_validate
description: Validate Plan Forge setup — check that all required files exist, file counts match preset expectations, and no unresolved placeholders remain.

name: forge_sweep
description: Run completeness sweep — scan code files for TODO, FIXME, HACK, stub, placeholder, and mock data markers. Returns locations of all deferred-work markers.

name: forge_status
description: Show all phases from DEPLOYMENT-ROADMAP.md with their current status (planned, in-progress, complete, paused).

NO_ROADMAP.recovery: Create docs/plans/DEPLOYMENT-ROADMAP.md or run pforge new-phase

name: forge_diff
description: Compare changed files against a plan's Scope Contract — detect drift, forbidden file edits, and unplanned changes.

plan.description: Path to the plan file (e.g., docs/plans/Phase-1-AUTH-PLAN.md)

name: forge_ext_search
description: Search the Plan Forge community extension catalog. Returns matching extensions with names, descriptions, categories, and install commands.

query.description: Search keyword (optional — omit to list all)

name: forge_ext_info
description: Show detailed information about a specific extension from the community catalog — author, version, category, provides, tags, and install command.

NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_ext_search to find available extensions

name: forge_new_phase
description: Create a new phase plan file and add it to the deployment roadmap. Returns the created file path and roadmap entry.

name: forge_analyze
description: Cross-artifact analysis — validates requirement traceability, test coverage, scope compliance, and validation gates. Returns a consistency score (0-100) with detailed breakdown. With quorum=true, dispatches to multiple AI models (including API providers like xAI Grok) for multi-model consensus analysis.

plan.description: Path to the plan or source file to analyze (e.g., docs/plans/Phase-1-AUTH-PLAN.md or src/services/billing.ts)

quorum.description: If true, dispatch analysis to multiple models and synthesize findings. Default: false

mode.description: Analysis mode: 'plan' (plan consistency) or 'file' (code review). Default: auto-detected from filename

models.description: Comma-separated model list override (e.g., 'grok-3-mini,claude-sonnet-4.6,gpt-5.3-codex'). Default: quorum config models

LOW_SCORE.recovery: Review gaps in traceability, coverage, tests, or gates

name: forge_diagnose
description: Multi-model bug investigation — dispatches independent bug analysis to multiple AI models (including API providers like xAI Grok), then synthesizes root cause analysis with fix recommendations. Each model examines code paths, failure modes, edge cases, and race conditions independently.

file.description: Path to the source file to investigate (e.g., src/services/billing.ts)

models.description: Comma-separated model list override (e.g., 'grok-3-mini,grok-4,claude-sonnet-4.6'). Default: quorum config models

name: forge_run_plan
description: Execute a hardened plan — spawn CLI workers for each slice, validate at every boundary, track tokens. Supports Full Auto (gh copilot CLI) and Assisted (human + automated gates) modes. Use --estimate for cost prediction without executing. Plans without crucibleId frontmatter are rejected — pass manualImport:true (with optional manualImportSource / manualImportReason) to bypass the Crucible gate; bypasses are logged to .forge/crucible/manual-imports.jsonl.

plan.description: Path to the hardened plan file (e.g., docs/plans/Phase-1-AUTH-PLAN.md)

mode.description: Execution mode: 'auto' (CLI worker) or 'assisted' (human + gates). Default: auto

model.description: Model override (e.g., claude-sonnet-4.6, gpt-5.2-codex). Default: auto

estimate.description: If true, return cost estimate without executing

resumeFrom.description: Slice number to resume from (skips completed slices)

dryRun.description: If true, parse and validate plan without executing

quorum.description: Quorum mode: 'false' (off), 'true' (all slices), 'auto' (threshold-based), 'power' (flagship models: Opus + GPT-5.3 + Grok 4.20), 'speed' (fast models: Sonnet + GPT-5.4-mini + Grok 4.1-fast). Default: auto

quorumThreshold.description: Override complexity threshold for auto quorum (1-10). Default: 6

manualImport.description: v2.37 Crucible — bypass the crucibleId frontmatter gate. Logged to .forge/crucible/manual-imports.jsonl.

manualImportSource.description: v2.37 Crucible — audit tag for --manual-import bypass. Default: human.

manualImportReason.description: v2.37 Crucible — optional free-form note recorded in the manual-import audit log.

prerequisites: gh copilot CLI installed (for auto mode)

quorum.description: Multi-model consensus: dispatch to 3+ models for dry-run analysis, synthesize best approach, then execute
Quorum mode (default: 'auto' — threshold-based; 'true' forces all slices; 'false' disables)

quorumThreshold.description: Complexity score threshold for auto mode (1-10, default: 6)

quorum.config: .forge.json → quorum { enabled, auto, threshold, models[], reviewerModel, dryRunTimeout }

PLAN_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Check the path or run forge_status to see available plans

NO_WORKER.recovery: Install gh copilot CLI, or use mode: 'assisted'

GATE_FAILED.recovery: Check slice results, fix code, use resumeFrom to continue

ABORTED.recovery: Re-run or use resumeFrom to continue from last slice

name: forge_abort
description: Abort the currently running plan execution. The abort takes effect between slices — the current slice will finish first.

NO_ACTIVE_RUN.message: No active plan execution to abort

name: forge_plan_status
description: Get the status of the latest plan execution run. Shows per-slice results, token usage, duration, and overall status from .forge/runs/.

plan.description: Filter by plan name (optional — shows latest if omitted)

name: forge_cost_report
description: Cost tracking report — shows total spend, per-model breakdown, and monthly aggregation from .forge/cost-history.json. Includes token counts, run history, and forge_model_stats (success rate per model from model-performance.json).

name: forge_estimate_quorum
description: Returns projected cost of a plan under all four quorum modes (auto / power / speed / false) in a single call. Agents MUST call this tool before presenting any dollar amount for a plan — hand-computed quorum costs drift by an order of magnitude. Backed by cost-service.mjs, the same code path that powers `pforge run-plan --estimate`.

planPath.description: Path to the plan Markdown file, relative to the project root.

resumeFrom.description: Optional slice number to start from — excludes already-shipped slices from the estimate.

PLAN_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Pass a valid planPath relative to the project root

PLAN_PARSE_FAILED.recovery: Run forge_analyze on the plan first to surface structural issues

agentGuidance: Call this tool before presenting any dollar amount or quorum-mode cost to the user. Do not hand-compute quorum costs in chat — the numbers you would invent drift quickly and have been observed to overshoot reality by an order of magnitude. This tool returns all four quorum modes (auto / power / speed / false) in one payload so you never need to make four separate calls or estimate.

name: forge_estimate_slice
description: Returns projected cost for a single slice under a chosen quorum mode. Cheaper than forge_estimate_quorum (which estimates the whole plan). Backed by cost-service.mjs estimateSlice(). Un-calibrated — no run-level historical correction factor applied.

planPath.description: Path to the plan Markdown file, relative to the project root.

sliceNumber.description: Slice identifier (numeric or alphanumeric, e.g. 4 or '2A').

PLAN_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Pass a valid planPath relative to the project root

SLICE_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Call forge_plan_status or forge_estimate_quorum to see available slice numbers

agentGuidance: Use this when you need cost for a single slice — cheaper than forge_estimate_quorum (which estimates the whole plan). Returns projected cost, complexity score, and a rationale for why the slice is or isn't quorum-eligible under the chosen mode.

name: forge_capabilities
description: Machine-readable API surface — returns all MCP tools with semantic metadata (intent, prerequisites, errors, cost), CLI commands, workflow graphs, config schema, dashboard info, and installed extensions. Agents call this once on session start for full discoverability.

name: forge_watch
description: WATCHER (v2.34) — read-only observer that tails another project's pforge run. Run this from a SECOND VS Code Copilot session with Plan-Forge as the workspace, pointing targetPath at the project being executed. Returns snapshot of current run state (slices passed/failed/in-progress, token counts, gate errors) plus heuristic anomaly detection. Mode 'analyze' additionally invokes a frontier model (default: claude-opus-4.7) for narrative advice. The watcher CANNOT modify any files in the target project.

targetPath.description: Absolute path to the project being watched (e.g., E:/GitHub/Rummag)

mode.description: snapshot = file reads only, no AI cost. analyze = invokes watcher model for advice.

tailEvents.description: Trailing events to include (1-200, default: 25). Lower = cheaper analyze prompts.

sinceTimestamp.description: (v2.35) ISO timestamp cursor — only flag events newer than this. Pass back the previous report's `cursor` field for continuous monitoring.

recordHistory.description: (v2.35) Append snapshot to watcher's own .forge/watch-history.jsonl (default: true)

sideEffects: in 'analyze' mode, invokes a frontier model (default claude-opus-4.7)
securityNote: Read-only by design — cannot modify any files in the target project. History is written only to watcher's own cwd.

MISSING_TARGET.recovery: Pass an absolute path to the project being watched

NO_RUNS.recovery: Verify the target has executed at least one pforge run

name: forge_watch_live
description: WATCHER LIVE TAIL (v2.35) — stream events from another project's pforge run for a fixed duration. Connects to the target's WebSocket hub if running (`.forge/server-ports.json`); falls back to file polling otherwise. Read-only by design — only subscribes, never sends commands. Returns aggregate stats and the captured event stream.

targetPath.description: Absolute path to the project being watched

durationMs.description: How long to listen, in ms (1000-3600000, default: 60000)

pollIntervalMs.description: Polling interval if hub not running (default: 3000ms)

sideEffects: opens read-only WebSocket subscription to target hub OR polls events.log
captures up to 500 events for the response payload
securityNote: Read-only subscriber — never sends commands or modifies target files.

MISSING_TARGET.recovery: Pass an absolute path to the project being watched

name: forge_memory_report
description: GX.3 (v2.36): aggregate the health of every memory surface — L2 jsonl files (record counts, schema _v distribution), OpenBrain queue state (pending/delivered/failed/deferred/DLQ), drain stats trend, capture telemetry (per-tool/per-type volume + dedup rate), search cache health, and orphans under .forge/. Read-only — never mutates files.
securityNote: Read-only aggregator. No data leaves the server.

name: forge_skill_status
description: Get recent skill execution events from the WebSocket hub history. Shows which skills were run, per-step results, and timing.

skillName.description: Filter by skill name (optional — shows all recent if omitted)

name: forge_run_skill
description: Execute a skill programmatically — parse the SKILL.md, run steps with validation gates, emit events to the hub, return structured results. Use for automated skill execution with progress tracking.

skill.description: Skill name (e.g., 'health-check', 'test-sweep') or path to SKILL.md

args.description: Arguments to pass to the skill (optional)

dryRun.description: If true, parse and validate skill without executing

sideEffects: may modify source files depending on skill

SKILL_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_skill_status to list available skills

name: forge_org_rules
description: Export org custom instructions — consolidate .github/instructions/*.instructions.md files into a single block for GitHub org-level Copilot custom instructions (Layer 1 of the two-layer model). Strips per-file frontmatter since org instructions apply universally. USE FOR: export org rules, generate org-level Copilot instructions, consolidate coding standards, org governance, GitHub org custom instructions.

format.description: Output format: github (default, plain text for org settings), markdown (formatted with headers), or json (structured)

output.description: File path to write output relative to project dir (optional — returns content if omitted)

name: forge_crucible_submit
description: Submit a raw idea to the Crucible — starts a new smelt (idea → spec workflow). Returns a smelt id, a recommended lane (tweak / feature / full), and the first interview question. USE FOR: kicking off Crucible from an AI agent or CLI with a one-line description of a change, bug, or feature. Agent-submitted smelts are subject to the recursion guardrail (default depth=1, set source='agent').

rawIdea.description: The raw idea text — a sentence or short paragraph describing the change.

lane.description: Override the recommended lane. Omit to accept the heuristic's choice.

source.description: Who submitted the smelt. Default: human.

parentSmeltId.description: Parent smelt id if this was spawned from another smelt (used for recursion depth tracking).

INVALID_LANE.recovery: Use tweak | feature | full, or omit to accept the heuristic

input.rawIdea: add rate limiting to the login endpoint

name: forge_crucible_ask
description: Advance the Crucible interview — supply an answer and get the next question, or mark the smelt ready for preview/finalize when the interview is complete. Call without `answer` to fetch the current question. USE FOR: the interactive Q&A loop that turns a raw idea into a hardened spec.

answer.description: Answer to the current question. Omit to fetch the current question without advancing.

SMELT_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Verify the smelt id via forge_crucible_list

WRONG_STATUS.message: smelt is finalized/abandoned, cannot continue interview
WRONG_STATUS.recovery: Start a new smelt with forge_crucible_submit

name: forge_crucible_preview
description: Render the current draft of a smelt as a Markdown plan. Returns the draft, the tentative phase name (null until finalized), and a list of unresolved fields (slots still awaiting an answer). USE FOR: reviewing before finalize, or for the dashboard's live-preview pane.

SMELT_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Verify the smelt id via forge_crucible_list

name: forge_crucible_finalize
description: Finalize a smelt — atomically claim the next phase number, write docs/plans/Phase-NN.md with a `crucibleId:` frontmatter stamp, and mark the smelt finalized. Returns the chosen phase name and the plan path. Plan Hardener handoff lands in Slice 01.6. USE FOR: closing the idea→spec workflow when the interview is done. Refuses to finalize when critical fields (scope-in/scope-files, validation-gates, forbidden-actions) are unresolved. Returns inferred build/test commands when a recognized manifest (package.json, *.csproj, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod) is present.

WRONG_STATUS.recovery: Only in-progress smelts can be finalized

PHASE_CLAIMED.recovery: Retry — the phase-claim race resolver will pick the next number

name: forge_crucible_list
description: List Crucible smelts, newest-first, optionally filtered by status. USE FOR: dashboard smelt-list panel, resuming in-progress smelts across sessions, auditing recently finalized smelts.

status.description: Filter by smelt status. Omit to return all.

name: forge_crucible_abandon
description: Abandon a smelt — marks it status=abandoned and releases any phase-number claim it held. Idempotent: re-abandoning a smelt is a no-op. USE FOR: discarding a smelt that was started by mistake or superseded by another idea.

name: forge_tempering_scan
description: Tempering (read-only) — scan an existing coverage report (lcov.info / coverage-final.json / cobertura.xml / jacoco.xml / go cover.out / tarpaulin JSON) and report per-layer coverage vs. configured minima. On first run, seeds .forge/tempering/config.json with enterprise defaults. Does NOT execute any tests. USE FOR: diagnosing coverage gaps, pre-deploy readiness checks, dashboard feeds. Writes .forge/tempering/scan-<ts>.json.

correlationId.description: Optional correlation id to thread this scan to an upstream smelt / plan / run. When omitted, one is minted.

sideEffects: seeds config.json with enterprise defaults on first run (never overwrites)
broadcasts tempering-scan-started and tempering-scan-completed hub events
captures scan summary to L3 via captureMemory (best-effort)

UNKNOWN_STACK.recovery: Ensure one of package.json / *.csproj / pyproject.toml / go.mod / Cargo.toml / pom.xml exists at the project root

NO_COVERAGE.message: No coverage report found for detected stack
NO_COVERAGE.recovery: Generate a coverage report with your test runner's coverage flag (e.g. vitest --coverage, dotnet test --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage", pytest --cov=. --cov-report=xml)

PARSE_EMPTY.message: Coverage report parsed to zero records
PARSE_EMPTY.recovery: Verify the report is non-empty and matches the expected format

name: forge_tempering_status
description: Return the latest N Tempering scan summaries — used by the dashboard feed and `forge_smith` panel. Read-only; never triggers a scan. USE FOR: checking freshness, listing recent coverage status, wiring Tempering into other tools.

name: forge_tempering_run
description: Tempering execution harness — runs the enabled test scanners (unit, integration, UI/Playwright, and API contract/OpenAPI/GraphQL) through each stack's preset adapter (typescript/dotnet/python/go/java/rust; php/swift/azure-iac stub until extension). Enforces config.runtimeBudgets with SIGTERM→SIGKILL, writes .forge/tempering/run-<ts>.json and .forge/tempering/artifacts/<runId>/contract/report.json, emits tempering-run-started / tempering-run-scanner-started / tempering-run-scanner-completed / tempering-run-completed hub events. USE FOR: post-slice verification, pre-deploy gates, CI adapters. Forbidden: does NOT edit source, does NOT create bugs (that lands in TEMPER-06), does NOT recurse into plan-forge itself.

correlationId.description: Optional correlation id to thread this run to an upstream smelt / plan / slice. When omitted, one is minted.

sliceRef.description: Optional plan+slice context. Surfaced in the run record and hub events so dashboards can group runs by slice.

lastGreenSha.description: Git SHA of the most recent green run; when present and config.execution.regressionFirst is true, the adapter is hinted to run tests covering changed files first.

fullMutation.description: Force mutation scanner regardless of scheduling gate. Default: false.

trigger.description: Trigger context for scheduling decisions (mutation scanner gating). Default: manual.

prerequisites: project stack is one of: typescript, dotnet, python, go, java, rust
sideEffects: broadcasts tempering-run-started / tempering-run-scanner-started / tempering-run-scanner-completed / tempering-run-completed hub events
captures an L3 memory entry on completion

MISSING_PROJECTDIR.recovery: Pass `path` or invoke from a project directory

NO_ADAPTER.message: No preset adapter for detected stack
NO_ADAPTER.recovery: Install the matching preset or extend presets/<stack>/tempering-adapter.mjs

name: forge_tempering_approve_baseline
description: Promote the current screenshot for a URL to the visual-diff baseline. Copies the latest screenshot to .forge/tempering/baselines/ and writes a JSON sidecar with promotion metadata. Idempotent — re-promoting overwrites. USE FOR: accepting intentional visual changes after a visual regression is flagged by forge_tempering_run.

urlHash.description: Hash of the URL to approve (from visual-diff scanner output)

url.description: Full URL to approve (alternative to urlHash — hash will be derived)

runId.description: Specific run ID to promote from (optional — defaults to most recent)

NO_SCREENSHOT.message: No screenshot found for the given hash
NO_SCREENSHOT.recovery: Run forge_tempering_run first to capture screenshots, then approve

ALREADY_BASELINE.recovery: This is informational — promotion is idempotent

INVALID_URL_HASH.recovery: Provide either urlHash from the visual-diff scanner output or the full URL

name: forge_tempering_drain
description: Tempering drain loop — wraps forge_tempering_run in a round-loop that re-probes until convergence or max-rounds cap fires. Writes per-round deltas to .forge/tempering/drain-history.jsonl and a final audit artifact to .forge/audits/dev-<ts>.json. USE FOR: recursive audit loops, post-plan convergence checks, drain-until-clean semantics. DO NOT USE FOR: single-shot tempering runs (use forge_tempering_run), editing source, creating bugs directly.

scanners.description: Scanner names to run each round (default: all enabled)

sideEffects: broadcasts drain-round-started / drain-round-completed / drain-converged hub events
may submit Crucible smelts for spec-lane findings

NO_SCANNERS.message: No scanners matched the requested set
NO_SCANNERS.recovery: Omit scanners param to run all available scanners

name: forge_triage_route
description: Triage a single tempering finding into one of three lanes: 'bug' (product defect), 'spec' (feature/spec gap), or 'classifier' (noise). Pure routing — no side effects. Fail-safe: unknown classifier output always routes to 'bug' with low confidence. USE FOR: per-finding triage after a tempering run, building custom drain loops. DO NOT USE FOR: batch triage (use forge_tempering_drain), registering bugs (use forge_bug_register after triage).

finding.description: A finding from a tempering scanner: { class, route, severity, evidence }

classifierResult.description: Output from the bug classifier. If omitted, fail-safe routes to 'bug' lane.

INVALID_FINDING.message: Finding object is required with at least a title field
INVALID_FINDING.recovery: Provide a finding object from a scanner result

name: forge_bug_register
description: Register a bug discovered by a tempering scanner. Classifies real-bug vs infra and writes .forge/bugs/<bugId>.json for real bugs. Infra-classified failures are not written to disk — they are only tracked in the run record.

correlationId.description: Correlation ID to thread to run/plan

sideEffects: captures L3 memory via captureMemory (real-bug only)

MISSING_EVIDENCE.message: Evidence must include testName or assertionMessage+stackTrace
MISSING_EVIDENCE.recovery: Provide at least testName in the evidence object

DUPLICATE_BUG.message: Bug with same fingerprint already exists
DUPLICATE_BUG.recovery: Use forge_bug_list to find the existing bug

CLASSIFIER_FAILED.message: Bug classifier could not determine classification
CLASSIFIER_FAILED.recovery: Manually set classification or retry with more evidence

SCANNER_NOT_RECOGNIZED.message: Scanner name not in known list
SCANNER_NOT_RECOGNIZED.recovery: Use one of the 9 supported scanner names

name: forge_bug_list
description: List bugs from the registry with optional filters. Returns all bugs matching the given criteria from .forge/bugs/.

since.description: Only bugs after this ISO date

until.description: Only bugs before this ISO date

name: forge_bug_update_status
description: Transition a bug's status (open → in-fix → fixed, or open → wont-fix/duplicate) with transition validation. Terminal states (fixed, wont-fix, duplicate) cannot be changed.

BUG_NOT_FOUND.message: Bug ID not found in registry
BUG_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_bug_list to find valid bug IDs

INVALID_TRANSITION.recovery: Check VALID_TRANSITIONS for allowed transitions from current status

name: forge_bug_validate_fix
description: Re-run the scanner(s) that discovered a bug to verify the fix. On pass: marks bug as 'fixed', dispatches commentValidatedFix to bug-adapter, broadcasts tempering-bug-validated-fixed event. On fail: appends attempt to bug.validationAttempts[], status unchanged.

testNameOverride.description: Override test name filter (default: uses bug.evidence.testName)

prerequisites: Scanner named by bug.scanner is available in tempering/scanners/
sideEffects: re-runs scanner(s) that discovered the bug
on pass: updates bug status to 'fixed'
on pass: dispatches commentValidatedFix to bug-adapter
on pass with OpenBrain configured: saves L3 thought

BUG_NOT_FOUND.message: Bug ID not found in registry
BUG_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_bug_list to find valid bug IDs

ALREADY_FIXED.recovery: No action needed — bug is already resolved

SCANNER_UNAVAILABLE.message: Scanner that discovered the bug is no longer registered
SCANNER_UNAVAILABLE.recovery: Use scannerOverride to specify an alternative scanner

name: forge_memory_capture
description: Capture a thought, decision, or lesson into OpenBrain persistent memory. USE FOR: recording architecture decisions, patterns chosen, gotchas discovered, conventions established, or any cross-session knowledge that future AI sessions should know. Requires OpenBrain to be configured in .vscode/mcp.json or .claude/mcp.json.

content.description: The thought, decision, or lesson to capture. Be specific — future agents will read this.

project.description: Project name to scope the memory (default: read from .forge.json)

type.description: Memory type: decision | lesson | convention | pattern | gotcha (default: decision)

name: forge_generate_image
description: Generate an image using AI image models (xAI Grok Aurora or OpenAI DALL-E). Provide a text description and get a generated image saved to disk. Supports format conversion — request WebP, PNG, AVIF, or JPEG regardless of what the API returns. Useful for creating logos, diagrams, UI mockups, icons, and illustrations during plan execution. Requires XAI_API_KEY (Grok) or OPENAI_API_KEY (DALL-E).

prompt.description: Detailed text description of the image to generate. Be specific about style, colors, composition, and content.

outputPath.description: File path to save the image (relative to project dir). The file extension determines the output format — e.g., 'assets/logo.webp' converts to WebP, 'docs/hero.png' converts to PNG.

model.description: Image model to use. Default: grok-imagine-image

format.description: Output format override (if different from file extension). Default: inferred from outputPath extension.

quality.description: Encoding quality 1-100. Default: 85. Lower = smaller file, less detail.

NO_API_KEY.message: No image generation API key found
NO_API_KEY.recovery: Set XAI_API_KEY or OPENAI_API_KEY in env or .forge/secrets.json

GENERATION_FAILED.recovery: Check the prompt, try a different model, or verify API key

name: forge_drift_report
description: Score the codebase against architecture guardrail rules. Tracks drift over time in .forge/drift-history.jsonl. Fires a bridge notification when score drops below threshold. With autoIncident, auto-captures incidents and generates fix proposals for high/critical violations.

threshold.description: Alert threshold 0-100. Fires drift-alert when score drops below this value. Default: 70

rules.description: Guardrail rule IDs to check. Default: all rules (empty-catch, any-type, sync-over-async, sql-injection, deferred-work)

autoIncident.description: Auto-capture incidents for high/critical violations and generate fix proposals. Default: false

NO_SOURCE_FILES.message: No source files found to analyze

name: forge_incident_capture
description: Capture an incident — record description, severity, affected files, and optional resolution timestamp for MTTR tracking. Appends to .forge/incidents.jsonl. Dispatches a bridge notification to the onCall target configured in .forge.json when an incident is captured.

description.description: Short description of the incident (e.g., 'API latency spike on /checkout')

resolvedAt.description: ISO 8601 resolution timestamp for MTTR calculation. Omit at capture time — supply via a second call when the incident is resolved.

prerequisites: .forge directory exists or will be created
sideEffects: may dispatch bridge notification to onCall target

RESOLVED_BEFORE_CAPTURED.recovery: Check the resolvedAt timestamp — it must be after the incident was captured

INVALID_SEVERITY.message: severity must be low, medium, high, or critical
INVALID_SEVERITY.recovery: Use one of: low, medium, high, critical

name: forge_regression_guard
description: Run regression guard — extract validation gate commands from plan files, execute them against the current codebase, and report passed/failed/blocked results. Guards against regressions when files change. Accepts a list of changed files and an optional plan to scope the check. Falls back to testCommand slice fields when no bash-block gates are present.

files.description: Changed file paths to guard (included in result for traceability). If omitted, all plan gates are checked.

plan.description: Path to a specific plan file to extract gates from (e.g., docs/plans/Phase-1-AUTH-PLAN.md). If omitted, all plan files in docs/plans/ are scanned.

failFast.description: If true, stop on first gate failure. Default: false

prerequisites: docs/plans/ contains at least one *-PLAN.md (or supply plan arg)
sideEffects: executes shell commands from validation gates

NO_PLANS_FOUND.message: No plan files found in docs/plans/
NO_PLANS_FOUND.recovery: Supply a plan path via the plan argument or create a plan file

GATE_FAILED.message: One or more validation gates failed
GATE_FAILED.recovery: Review failed gate output and fix the issue, then re-run

name: forge_runbook
description: Generate a human-readable operational runbook from a hardened plan file. Parses slices, scope contract, build/test commands, and validation gates into a structured Markdown document. Optionally appends recent incidents from .forge/incidents.jsonl for operational context. Saves to .forge/runbooks/<plan-name>-runbook.md and returns the output path.

plan.description: Path to the plan file (e.g., docs/plans/Phase-1-AUTH-PLAN.md)

includeIncidents.description: Include recent incidents from .forge/incidents.jsonl for operational context. Default: true

name: forge_hotspot
description: Identify git churn hotspots — files that change most frequently. Helps prioritize refactoring, testing, and review effort. Caches results in .forge/hotspot-cache.json (24h TTL). Accepts --top N and --since filters.

top.description: Number of hotspot files to return. Default: 10

since.description: Git log --since filter (e.g., '3 months ago', '2024-01-01'). Default: '6 months ago'

GIT_LOG_FAILED.recovery: Ensure you are inside a git repository with commit history

NO_COMMITS.message: No commits found in the given time range
NO_COMMITS.recovery: Widen the --since filter or check the branch has history

name: forge_health_trend
description: Health trend analysis — aggregates drift scores, cost history, incident frequency, and model performance over a configurable time window. Returns per-metric summaries, an overall health score (0–100), and trend direction. Data sourced from .forge/ operational files.

days.description: Number of days of history to analyze. Default: 30

NO_DATA.message: No operational data found for the requested time window
NO_DATA.recovery: Run forge tools (drift, run-plan, incident) to generate data, or widen the --days window

name: forge_alert_triage
description: Triage open alerts — read incidents and drift violations, rank by priority (severity × recency), and return a prioritized list. Read-only: does not modify any data store. Tiebreak: more recent alerts rank higher when priority scores are equal.

minSeverity.description: Minimum severity to include. Default: low (all)

max.description: Maximum number of alerts to return. Default: 20

NO_ALERTS.message: No open alerts found matching the filter criteria
NO_ALERTS.recovery: Lower --min-severity or check that incidents exist in .forge/incidents.jsonl

INVALID_SEVERITY.message: minSeverity must be low, medium, high, or critical
INVALID_SEVERITY.recovery: Use one of: low, medium, high, critical

notes: Read-only — does not write to any data store. Priority = severity_weight × recency_factor. Tiebreak: more recent timestamp ranks higher.

name: forge_deploy_journal
description: Record a deployment — log version, deployer, optional notes, and optional slice reference. Appends to .forge/deploy-journal.jsonl. Used by forge_incident_capture to correlate incidents with the most recent deploy.

by.description: Who or what triggered the deploy (e.g., 'CI', 'alice'). Default: 'unknown'

notes.description: Free-form deploy notes (e.g., 'hotfix for checkout timeout')

prerequisites: .forge directory exists or will be created

name: forge_dep_watch
description: Scan project dependencies for known vulnerabilities using npm audit. Compares against previous snapshot in .forge/deps-snapshot.json to detect new and resolved CVEs. Fires a bridge notification when new vulnerabilities are found. Non-npm projects degrade gracefully.

notify.description: Send bridge notification for new vulnerabilities. Default: true

prerequisites: package.json or package-lock.json in project root

NO_PACKAGE_JSON.message: No package.json found in project root
NO_PACKAGE_JSON.recovery: Ensure you are in a Node.js project with a package.json file

AUDIT_FAILED.recovery: Check npm is installed and network connectivity; non-npm projects return graceful degradation

name: forge_secret_scan
description: Post-commit entropy analysis — scan git diff output for high-entropy strings that may be leaked secrets. Uses Shannon entropy with key-name heuristics. Never logs actual secret values — only file paths, line numbers, entropy scores, and <REDACTED> placeholders. Caches results in .forge/secret-scan-cache.json. Annotates deploy journal sidecar when last deploy matches HEAD.

since.description: Git ref or range to scan (e.g., 'HEAD~1', 'abc123..def456'). Default: HEAD~1

threshold.description: Minimum Shannon entropy to flag (3.5–5.0). Default: 4.0

securityNote: Never logs actual secret values — only file paths, line numbers, entropy scores, and <REDACTED> placeholders. All findings are masked before caching or emitting telemetry.

GIT_UNAVAILABLE.message: git is not available or not a git repository
GIT_UNAVAILABLE.recovery: Ensure you are inside a git repository — tool degrades gracefully returning { clean: null, scannedFiles: 0, findings: [], error: 'git unavailable' }

DIFF_TIMEOUT.recovery: Narrow the --since range to reduce diff size

name: forge_env_diff
description: Compare environment variable keys across .env files — detect missing keys between baseline and target environments. Compares key names only (never values). Caches results in .forge/env-diff-cache.json. Integrates with forge_runbook to surface environment key gaps.

baseline.description: Baseline .env file path (relative to project root). Default: .env

files.description: Comma-separated target .env file paths to compare against baseline (e.g., '.env.staging,.env.production'). Default: auto-detect .env.* files

sideEffects: writes .forge/env-diff-cache.json — key names only, no values
securityNote: Compares key names only — never reads, logs, or caches environment variable values.

BASELINE_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Create the baseline file or pass --baseline pointing to an existing .env file

TARGET_NOT_FOUND.message: one or more target .env files not found
TARGET_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Check the --files argument or ensure .env.* files exist in the project root

name: forge_fix_proposal
description: Generate a 1-3 slice fix plan from regression, drift, incident, secret-scan, Crucible (stalled/orphan), or tempering-bug failure. Writes to docs/plans/auto/LIVEGUARD-FIX-<id>.md. Capped at one proposal per id.

source.description: Data source: 'regression', 'drift', 'incident', 'secret', 'crucible', or 'tempering-bug'. Default: auto-detect from latest data

incidentId.description: Incident ID to generate fix for (required for incident source)

smeltId.description: Crucible smelt ID to target (optional for crucible source; auto-selects worst offender when omitted)

bugId.description: Bug registry ID (required when source is 'tempering-bug')

prerequisites: LiveGuard data available (drift, incidents, secret scan, Crucible funnel, or tempering bugs)
securityNote: Plans are generated locally. One proposal per incidentId to prevent spam.

NO_DATA.recovery: Run drift, incident-capture, regression-guard, secret-scan, start a Crucible smelt, or register a tempering bug first

ALREADY_EXISTS.message: Fix proposal already exists for this ID

CRUCIBLE_HEALTHY.message: Crucible funnel is healthy — no stalled or orphan smelts to fix
CRUCIBLE_HEALTHY.recovery: Wait for the watcher to flag a smelt, or pass a specific smeltId to force a proposal

MISSING_BUG_ID.message: bugId is required when source is tempering-bug

BUG_TERMINAL_STATUS.message: Bug is already in a terminal status (fixed/wont-fix/duplicate)
BUG_TERMINAL_STATUS.recovery: No fix plan needed — bug is already resolved

name: forge_quorum_analyze
description: Assemble a structured 3-section quorum prompt from any LiveGuard data source. No LLM calls — returns the prompt for multi-model dispatch. Supports customQuestion freeform override (max 500 chars) and analysisGoal presets.

targetFile.description: Specific data file path (relative to .forge/). Defaults to most recent for source type.

analysisGoal.description: Preset question: 'root-cause', 'risk-assess', 'fix-review', 'runbook-validate'. Ignored when customQuestion is provided.

customQuestion.description: Freeform question override (max 500 chars). Replaces the analysisGoal preset entirely.

quorumSize.description: Number of model votes to request in the prompt. Default: 3.

sideEffects: none — read-only prompt assembly, no LLM calls
securityNote: customQuestion is XSS-validated and length-capped at 500 chars. No data leaves the server.

XSS_DETECTED.recovery: Remove script tags or event handlers

input.customQuestion: Why did drift score drop 15 points?

output.quorumPrompt:
## Context
...

## Question
Why did drift score drop 15 points?

## Voting Instruction
...
output.questionUsed: Why did drift score drop 15 points?

name: forge_liveguard_run
description: Run all applicable LiveGuard checks in a single call and return a unified health report. Executes: drift, sweep, secret-scan, regression-guard, dep-watch, alert-triage, and health-trend. Optionally runs diff if a plan is specified. NOTE: May take 2-3 minutes for .NET projects (dep-watch runs `dotnet list package --vulnerable`). Set client timeout to at least 300 seconds.

plan.description: Plan file path for scope diff (optional). If omitted, diff is skipped.

sideEffects: runs drift scan, sweep, secret scan, regression guard, dep watch, alert triage, health trend in sequence
securityNote: Composite tool — executes multiple LiveGuard tools. No data leaves the server.

PARTIAL_FAILURE.recovery: Check individual tool errors in the response

name: forge_home_snapshot
description: Read-only aggregated snapshot of the four shop-floor subsystems (Crucible, active runs, LiveGuard, Tempering) plus a trimmed activity feed. Use as a one-call health overview.

activityTail.description: Recent hub events to include (default: 25, clamped 1..200)

securityNote: Read-only aggregator. No data leaves the server.

IO_FAILURE.recovery: Check file permissions and .forge directory integrity

name: forge_review_add
description: Add an item to the review queue. Used by producers (crucible/tempering/bug classifier) when human judgment is required.

ERR_INVALID_SOURCE.recovery: Use one of: crucible-stall, tempering-quorum-inconclusive, tempering-baseline, bug-classify, fix-plan-approval

ERR_INVALID_SEVERITY.recovery: Use one of: blocker, high, medium, low

ERR_INVALID_TITLE.message: Title is required and must be non-empty

ERR_INVALID_CONTEXT.recovery: Pass context as a JSON object, not a string

name: forge_review_list
description: List review queue items with optional filters and pagination.

name: forge_review_resolve
description: Resolve an open review queue item (approve/reject/defer). Emits hub event and captures L3 memory.
prerequisites: Review item exists and is open
sideEffects: updates review item status to resolved/deferred
captures L3 memory via captureMemory (structured tags only, no free-text note)

ERR_ITEM_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_review_list to find valid item IDs

ERR_ALREADY_RESOLVED.message: Item is already resolved or deferred
ERR_ALREADY_RESOLVED.recovery: No action needed — item is already resolved

ERR_INVALID_RESOLUTION.recovery: Use one of: approve, reject, defer

ERR_INVALID_RESOLVED_BY.message: resolvedBy is required and must be non-empty
ERR_INVALID_RESOLVED_BY.recovery: Provide the name/identifier of who resolved it

name: forge_delegate_to_agent
description: Route a tempering bug to the appropriate agent/skill for read-only analysis.
sideEffects: creates review queue item when mode=review-queue-item
captures L3 memory (metadata only — bugId, agent, severity)

BUG_NOT_FOUND.recovery: Use forge_bug_list to find valid bug IDs

NO_ROUTE.message: No routing rule matches this bug type/severity
NO_ROUTE.recovery: Bug may need manual triage — check type and severity

name: forge_notify_send
description: Send a notification directly via a named adapter, bypassing routing rules. Use for ad-hoc agent dispatches.
sideEffects: sends HTTP request to configured adapter endpoint
emits notification-sent or notification-send-failed hub event

ERR_LITERAL_SECRET.recovery: Use ${env:VAR_NAME} template instead of literal URLs

name: forge_notify_test
description: Test notification adapter configuration. Validates config and optionally sends a test payload.

dryRun.description: If true, only validate config without sending (default: true)

sideEffects: optionally sends test HTTP request when dryRun=false

name: forge_search
description: Search across forge artifacts — runs, bugs, incidents, tempering, hub events, review queue, memories, and plans. Reads existing L2 files and optional L3 OpenBrain index. Returns ranked results with snippets.

tags.description: Filter: hit must have ALL listed tags

correlationId.description: Filter + score boost on exact match

sources.description: Limit to source types (run, bug, incident, tempering, hub-event, review, memory, plan)

name: forge_timeline
description: Unified chronological view across all forge event sources with correlationId grouping. Merges hub-events, runs, memories, openbrain, watch, tempering, bugs, and incidents into a single timeline.

from.description: Start of window: ISO timestamp or relative (24h, 7d, 30m). Default: now - 24h

to.description: End of window: ISO timestamp or relative. Default: now

correlationId.description: Filter to a single correlation thread

sources.description: Limit to source types: hub-event, run, memory, openbrain, watch, tempering, bug, incident

events.description: Filter by event type (glob supported: slice-*, tempering-*)

groupBy.description: Default: time (flat chronological). correlation: group by correlationId

name: forge_doctor_quorum
description: Preflight quorum viability check — probes all models in a preset against the current runtime and reports availability, synthesis viability, and fallback recommendations.

preset.description: Quorum preset to check. 'all' checks both presets. Default: all

name: forge_testbed_run
description: Run a testbed scenario against an external testbed repository. Executes preflight checks, setup, execution steps, and assertions, then writes defect findings. Use dryRun to validate without executing.

scenarioId.description: Scenario fixture ID (filename stem under docs/plans/testbed-scenarios/)

testbedPath.description: Path to testbed repository (default: from .forge.json testbed.path)

dryRun.description: If true, skip execute and teardown steps (default: false)

ERR_TESTBED_DIRTY.recovery: Commit or stash changes in testbed

ERR_TESTBED_HEAD_MISMATCH.message: Testbed HEAD does not match expected commit

name: forge_master_ask
description: Ask Forge-Master to reason about Plan Forge workflows — ideate features via Crucible, troubleshoot failures, query run status, or get operational guidance. Classifies intent, fetches memory context, and orchestrates read-only tool calls. Returns reply text, tool call history, token counts, and session ID for conversation continuity.

message.description: Your question or request for Forge-Master

sessionId.description: Session ID for conversation continuity (omit for new session)

maxToolCalls.description: Max tool calls per turn (default: 5, max: 10)

agentGuidance: Use forge_master_ask for multi-step reasoning about Plan Forge workflows — ideating features, troubleshooting failures, querying run status, or funneling ideas into Crucible smelts. Forge-Master classifies intent, fetches memory context, and orchestrates tool calls on your behalf. Prefer this over manually calling individual forge tools when the task is open-ended or involves multiple steps. Do NOT use for direct file edits or code generation — Forge-Master is read-only.

reasoning_model_unavailable.recovery: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or XAI_API_KEY, or configure forgeMaster.reasoningModel in .forge.json

input.message: I want to add multi-tenant billing to my pipeline

output.reply: I've started a Crucible smelt for your multi-tenant billing feature...

name: forge_testbed_findings
description: Query testbed defect-log findings. Returns findings filtered by status, severity, or date. Read-only — does not modify any files.

status.description: Filter by status (open, fixed, wontfix, duplicate)

severity.description: Filter by severity (blocker, high, medium, low, polish)

since.description: Filter by date — only findings on or after this ISO 8601 date (e.g. 2026-04-01)

name: forge_testbed_happypath
description: Run all happy-path testbed scenarios sequentially. Returns aggregated pass/fail results with per-scenario details. Use dryRun to validate without executing.

testbedPath.description: Path to testbed repository (default: from .forge.json testbed.path)

dryRun.description: If true, skip execute and teardown steps (default: false)

name: forge_meta_bug_file
description: File a self-repair meta-bug against Plan Forge itself. Creates (or deduplicates) a GitHub issue for plan, orchestrator, or prompt defects discovered during execution. Auto-attaches trajectory context when slice reference is provided.

symptom.description: Observable symptom that revealed the defect

filePaths.description: Files affected by or related to the defect

slice.description: Slice reference (e.g. '3') — triggers auto-pull of trajectory excerpt

plan.description: Plan name or path for context

produces: GitHub issue (or comment on existing issue)
sideEffects: creates or comments on GitHub issue in self-repair repo

NO_TOKEN.recovery: Set GITHUB_TOKEN env var, add to .forge/secrets.json, or authenticate with gh CLI

CREATE_FAILED.recovery: Check token permissions and repository access

INVALID_CLASS.message: Bug class not in META_BUG_CLASSES enum
INVALID_CLASS.recovery: Use one of: plan-defect, orchestrator-defect, prompt-defect

name: forge_graph_query
description: Query the Plan Forge knowledge graph. Returns subgraph of nodes and edges matching the query. Supports phase, file, recent-changes, and neighbor traversal queries.

filter.description: Phase name, file path, node ID, or keyword depending on query type

since.description: ISO timestamp or relative (90d, 30d, 7d) for recent-changes queries

ERR_BAD_TYPE.recovery: Use phase, file, recent-changes, or neighbors

name: forge_patterns_list
description: List recurring patterns detected across plan runs. Returns gate-failure recurrences, model failure rates, slice flap patterns, and cost anomalies. Advisory only — never injected into plan hardener or executor.

since.description: ISO timestamp — only patterns observed after this date (optional)
